title: senecio erraticus subsp. barbareifolius
intro: Senecio erraticus subsp. barbareifolius, also known as Barbary groundsel or Barbary ragwort, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Senecio erraticus subsp. barbareifolius include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent to soothe skin irritations. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
